3.2 Capacity of participants and consortium as a whole We, the CO2HESION consortium, consist of a complementary team of research, technology, industrial, and innovation ecosystem partners covering the full value chain required to develop and validate an integrated CO2 capture, purification, and utilisation (CCU) system for the production of competitive chemical products. We combine expertise in carbon capture, catalytic conversion, reactor and process engineering, industrial integration, digitalisation, system optimisation, life cycle assessment, and techno-economic analysis. Complementarity and Value Chain Coverage: We cover the full CCU value chain, from CO2 generation and capture to conversion, validation, and end-use. Industrial CO2 streams are provided by TOR, enabling real operating conditions from the outset. Capture, purification, and system integration are addressed by TRSA, supported by material development and catalytic conversion from UNIZAR and ACC. Process validation and industrial compatibility are ensured through ORL and TOR, covering both petrochemical and ceramic applications. System optimisation, monitoring, and scale-up are supported by IRIS through Digital Twin and data-driven approaches, while INEGI provides techno-economic, environmental, and sustainability assessment. ADM brings expertise in the implementation of the EU Innovation Fund’s GHG Avoidance Methodology. Exploitation and market alignment are driven by ICP, ADM, and BOP, ensuring that technical results translate into deployable solutions, along with standardisation activities (UNE). Industrial and Exploitation Dimension: We include strong industrial participation, ensuring a credible pathway towards deployment and commercialisation. TRSA provides engineering and integration capabilities for large-scale industrial systems, while TOR acts as the industrial host, providing the CO2 source, hosting the pilot implementation, and enabling validation of both use cases under real operating conditions. ORL ensures alignment with real market needs in petrochemicals. IRIS strengthens the industrial dimension through advanced monitoring, Digital Twin, and data-driven optimisation capabilities, directly supporting scalable and competitive operation. ACC contributes to circular economy integration through battery recycling value chains, enabling CO2 utilisation in secondary raw material recovery. INEGI supports industrial decision-making through techno-economic and environmental validation, while ICP and BOP ensure that exploitation pathways, IP management, and market positioning (ADM) and standardisation (UNE) are addressed from early stages. 3.2.1 Consortium as a whole  
Our consortium involves partners from 12 countries: Italy, Greece, Germany, Spain, Portugal, Poland, Cyprus, Serbia, Ireland, Ukraine, Turkey, and the United Kingdom.  
9 Universities and Research Institutes: We, Politecnico di Milano (PoliMi), are the coordinator of RePaintIT, with extensive experience in coordinating large scale European projects (e.g. LightMe, an OITB project with 27 partners). The strong industrial involvement of RePaintIT ensures broad coverage of the value chain, from paint production and waste generation to processing and final material use. We, PPG, define industrial requirements and validate outputs in paint manufacturing, while CRF and Farplas ensure applicability in automotive and solvent-based coating environments. Torrecid and SBT extend validation to ceramics and polymer-based products, enabling cross-sector integration of recovered materials. These activities are supported by SMEs providing pilot lines, monitoring systems, digital tools, and optimisation frameworks, ensuring that solutions are not only technically validated but also operationally and economically viable. This structure is directly aligned with our project’s exploitation strategy, as defined in Section 2.2. Each industrial partner is positioned as a primary exploiter of specific results: PPG for reintegration into paint formulations, CRF and Farplas for coating processes, and Torrecid and SBT for secondary raw material applications. This ensures that validated outputs have immediate pathways to market within existing industrial value chains. In parallel, technology providers and SMEs (e.g. Captive, Tecnom, IRIS, RDC, AIMEN) will exploit the developed processing technologies, monitoring systems, and digital solutions as standalone or integrated commercial offerings. The involvement of IoCT further supports sector-wide uptake, while INC drives business modelling and exploitation planning. By embedding end-users, technology providers, and exploitation partners within our consortium, RePaintIT ensures that all key results are linked to clear ownership, business models, and deployment pathways. This guarantees consistency between technical development and exploitation measures, reducing time-to-market and maximising the impact and scalability of our project outcomes.

3.2 Capacity of participants and consortium as a whole We, the H2SAVE consortium, are composed of a complementary set of academic, research, technology, and industrial partners covering the full value chain required to develop and validate a reversible hydrogen-based energy storage system for critical infrastructures. Our team combines expertise in electrochemical materials and cell development, stack engineering, thermal system design, gas handling, digitalisation, system integration, safety, life cycle assessment, and techno-economic assessment. At the core of our technological development, we have the Spanish National Research Council (CSIC) and Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology (GIST) leading the development of reversible protonic ceramic electrochemical cells, including materials optimisation, interface engineering, and single-cell testing. Torrecid Group (TOR) contributes to electrode development, current collectors, and manufacturability aspects, ensuring that materials innovations are compatible with scalable processing routes. Stack design, fabrication, and validation are led by Técnicas Reunidas (TR SA), supported by CSIC, ensuring a fluid transition from cell-level performance to stack-level operation and alignment with industrial engineering practices. Thermal integration and hotbox design are addressed by SINTEF, bringing strong expertise in high-temperature systems, thermal management, and industrial system design. Multiphysics modelling of the stack and hotbox is carried out by CSIC, providing predictive understanding of electrochemical, thermal, and mechanical behaviour. System-level design, life cycle assessment, and techno-economic feasibility are supported by TR SA, CSIC, and European Institute for Energy Research (EIFER), using ASPEN-based modelling to evaluate large-scale storage scenarios and system performance. EIFER also plays a central role in hybrid system integration and energy system modelling. Digitalisation and intelligent system operation are driven by IRIS Technology Solutions (IRIS), which leads the development of advanced monitoring, control, and Digital Twin capabilities, supported by SINTEF and TRSA. This includes high-temperature sensing, real-time diagnostics, and system-level modelling to ensure safe, stable, and controllable operation. We also integrate application-level validation through the Kyiv City Clinical Hospital №12 (Ukraine), which will contribute to defining realistic critical infrastructure scenarios, particularly in the context of energy resilience and emergency response. This ensures that our system validation is grounded in real operational needs and aligned with the call’s emphasis on supporting resilience and reconstruction contexts. Safety, regulatory compliance, and risk management are addressed by EIFER and PROACT, ensuring that safety-by-design principles, HAZID/HAZOP methodologies, and pre-standardisation activities are embedded throughout our project. These partners also support regulatory alignment and future certification pathways. Access to Critical Infrastructure and Validation Environments: We ensure access to representative critical infrastructure conditions through a combination of experimental facilities, modelling environments, and use-case partners. Laboratory-scale and subsystem validation will be performed using advanced facilities available at TRSA, SINTEF, CSIC, and GIST, including high-temperature testing rigs, electrochemical characterisation platforms, and system integration environments. Realistic operational conditions for critical infrastructures (e.g., hospital load profiles, emergency scenarios, and off-grid operation) will be reproduced through simulation, emulation, and controlled experimental setups. The Kyiv City Clinical Hospital №12 (Ukrainian) will provide access to real-world data, operational constraints, and resilience scenarios, ensuring that our validation activities reflect actual infrastructure needs, particularly in disrupted or energy-constrained environments. Complementarity and Value Chain Coverage: We cover the full value chain: Materials and cells (CSIC, GIST); Component and manufacturing development (TOR); Stack and subsystem engineering (TRSA, CSIC); Thermal systems and integration (SINTEF); System modelling, TEA, LCA (TRSA, CSIC, EIFER); Digital Twin, monitoring, and control (IRIS, SINTEF, TRSA); System integration and hybrid energy architecture (EIFER, TRSA); Application validation and use-case definition (KYIVH); Safety, regulations, and standardisation (EIFER, PROACT). This composition will ensure that each stage of our development is supported by partners with the required expertise while maintaining strong interconnections between activities. Industrial and Exploitation Dimension: We include strong industrial participation, ensuring a credible pathway towards exploitation. TRSA provides engineering and system integration expertise relevant to large-scale deployment, while TOR contributes advanced materials, processing, and manufacturing capabilities for ceramic-based components and interfaces relevant to stack and subsystem development. IRIS, as an advanced engineering company from the process monitoring and digitalisation space, strengthens our industrial dimension through its expertise in sensing, diagnostics, Digital Twins, data-driven optimisation, and Industry 4.0 integration, all of which are directly relevant to future deployment and commercialisation of the H2SAVE solution. EIFER provides expertise in energy systems integration, energy management systems (EMS), and techno-economic and environmental assessment (TEA/LCA), supporting system-level optimisation and business case definition. PROACT contributes expertise in safety and regulatory frameworks, critical for deployment in real infrastructures. This cohesive industrial collaboration with important companies from their sectors will ensure that our project results can be further scaled up in the post-project phase and that there are clear pathways towards industrial uptake and commercialisation.
